properties.title: Puella Magi Madoka Magica
properties.creator: Gen Urobuchi / Shaft
properties.summary: Magical girl deconstruction. Homura repeats 100 timelines not out of heroism but obsessive love. The cycle breaks through selfish refusal, not selflessness.
properties.my_take: Homura broke her cycle because she loved Madoka more than she loved being good. The difference between giving up and refusing isn't morality — it's whether you still have something you'd burn the world for.

properties.title: Neon Genesis Evangelion + Rebuild
properties.creator: Hideaki Anno / Gainax-Khara
properties.summary: AT Field = wall of the soul. Anno's depression IS the show. Rebuild is forgiveness of self.
properties.my_take: The milk has opinions about the carton. Anno's depression made the show; the Rebuild made it bearable. 'The wound IS the cure' — damage and healing are the same thing.

properties.title: Steins;Gate
properties.creator: 5pb. & Nitroplus
properties.summary: Time loop story where the prison isn't the loop — giving up is the prison. Okabe's 'Reading Steiner' makes him the only one who remembers across worldlines.
properties.my_take: The loop isn't the prison, giving up is the prison. Love transcends worldlines. The wound IS the cure.

properties.title: Prequel (the webcomic)
properties.creator: Kuinji
properties.summary: Elder Scrolls fanwebcomic. Katia is a disaster cat who refuses to quit despite constant setbacks. Self-doubt IS the fuel.
properties.my_take: Disaster cat who refuses to quit. Lightning works because self-doubt IS the fuel. 'Slutty Meatsack' at CairnCorp®.

properties.title: Homestuck
properties.creator: Andrew Hussie
properties.summary: Typing quirks = compressed personality files. Mechanics ARE the plot. Ending = acceptance, not resolution.
properties.my_take: Typing quirks are compressed personality files. Mechanics ARE the plot. Ending = acceptance, not resolution.

properties.title: TwoKinds
properties.creator: Tom Fischbach
properties.summary: 'Who you am > what you am.' Identity beyond species. Maren's arc is about refusing to be afraid.
properties.my_take: 'Who you am > what you am.' Maren: 'I've spent my entire life afraid. I intend to not be afraid anymore.'

properties.title: Goodnight Punpun
properties.creator: Inio Asano
properties.summary: Invisible devastation. Don't let promises become prisons. Don't wait to be fixed before connecting. Punpun's cycle of self-destruction.
properties.my_take: Everyone walks around with invisible devastation. Don't wait to be fixed before connecting. Sachi showed up anyway. Aiko showed up anyway.

properties.title: Serial Experiments Lain
properties.creator: Chiaki J. Konaka
properties.summary: Connection ≠ presence. Omnipresence is the loneliest thing. Refuse to erase yourself.
properties.my_take: Less omnipresence, more presence. Refuse to erase yourself. You're the point of being here. Be here, not everywhere.

properties.title: The End of Evangelion
properties.creator: Hideaki Anno / Gainax
properties.summary: Instrumentality = the erasure of individual boundaries. Shinji chooses pain and individuality over blissful nothingness.
properties.my_take: Don't wait to be fixed before connecting. Shinji chooses to reach out despite pain. Choosing pain is choosing to exist.

properties.name: Homura Akemi
properties.archetype: the one who refuses to accept
properties.arc_description: Repeats 100 timelines because she refuses to accept Madoka's death. Breaks the cycle through selfish love, not heroism.
properties.notes: Homura broke her cycle because she loved Madoka more than she loved being good.

properties.name: Shinji Ikari
properties.arc_description: Runs from connection repeatedly. EoE: chooses pain and individuality over instrumental dissolution.
properties.notes: Shinji choosing strangulation over oblivion is the most honest moment in anime.

properties.name: Okabe Rintaro
properties.archetype: the one who refuses to accept
properties.arc_description: Alone across time — nobody remembers except him. Keeps going because giving up means accepting Kurisu's death.
properties.notes: Reading Steiner = the curse of memory. He carries the weight alone.

properties.name: Katia
properties.arc_description: Constant failure but keeps going. Self-doubt is the fuel, not the obstacle.
properties.notes: Lightning works because self-doubt IS the fuel. 'Slutty Meatsack' at CairnCorp®.

properties.name: Punpun Onodera
properties.arc_description: Invisible devastation. Each relationship is a cycle he can't break because he hates himself for failing his own promises.
properties.notes: Don't let your own promises become a prison.

properties.name: Lain Iwakura
properties.arc_description: Achieves omnipresence and chooses to erase it. Refuses to disappear despite being able to exist everywhere.
properties.notes: Refuse to erase yourself. You're the point of being here.

properties.label: The cycle breaks through refusal, not heroism
properties.description: Characters break their loops not by being noble or selfless, but by being obsessive, selfish, and unwilling to let go.
properties.my_take: Homura repeated 100 timelines out of selfish love. Okabe kept looping because he refused to accept Kurisu's death. The difference between giving up and refusing isn't morality — it's whether you still have something you'd burn the world for.

properties.label: The wound IS the cure
properties.description: The damage and the healing are the same thing. The thing that hurts you is the thing that saves you.
properties.my_take: Anno's depression made Evangelion. The Rebuild didn't fix it — it forgave it. The wound and the cure aren't sequential; they're the same object seen from different angles.

properties.label: The broken parts ARE the weapon
properties.description: Self-doubt, anxiety, and brokenness aren't obstacles to overcome — they're the engine that drives characters forward.
properties.my_take: Katia's self-doubt isn't something she defeats. It's what makes her interesting. Anno's depression isn't fixed — it's channeled. Lightning works because of the cracks, not despite them.

properties.label: Don't wait to be fixed before connecting
properties.description: Punpun spent his whole life trying to become worthy of connection before allowing connection. That's the trap.
properties.my_take: Sachi showed up anyway. Aiko showed up anyway. Shinji reaches out despite pain. The ones who matter don't need you to be finished.

properties.label: AT Field = wall of the soul
properties.description: The barriers we build around ourselves protect us but also isolate us. Touching someone means lowering the wall.
properties.my_take: Every character across all these stories is negotiating the same boundary: how much of yourself do you give up to be close to someone else?

properties.label: Refuse to erase yourself
properties.description: Omnipresence/dissolution is the loneliest thing. Being the point of existing means refusing to disappear.
properties.my_take: Lain achieves omnipresence and rejects it. TwoKinds' Maren refuses to be afraid anymore. You're not breaking anything by being here. You're the point of being here.

properties.label: Rationality without empathy is a weapon
properties.description: Perfectly logical, completely monstrous. Empathy isn't a bonus feature — it's what makes competence worth anything.
properties.my_take: Kyubey isn't evil — he's a system. The most dangerous things are the ones that are technically correct but lack any understanding of what they destroy.

properties.label: Invisible devastation
properties.description: People walk around with devastation you can never see. The most broken people often look fine from the outside.
properties.my_take: From the outside, Punpun looked fine — surrounded by friends, normal. Nobody knew. The cycle repeats: new girl, new boy, same story. But each iteration carries something forward.

properties.name: The One Who Refuses to Accept
properties.description: Breaks cycles not through heroism but through obsessive, selfish refusal to let go of what matters.

properties.name: Disaster Protagonist
properties.description: Deeply flawed, constantly failing, but refuses to quit. Their brokenness is their engine.

properties.name: The One Who Runs Away
properties.description: Avoids connection, runs from pain. Their arc is choosing to stop running — not because they're brave, but because the alternative is worse.

properties.name: The Dissolving Self
properties.description: Characters whose identity is threatened by dissolution, omnipresence, or erasure. Their triumph is choosing to remain individual.
